# 

# **RULEBOOK** **BC GAME MASTERS CHAMPIONSHIP \#2**

---

### **Index**

1. General Provisions  
2. Registrations  
3. Tournament Format  
   3.1 Group stage scoring system  
   3.2 Group stage tiebreaker  
   3.3 Format  
4. Tournament Schedule  
5. Settings and Maps  
    4.1. Server Configuration  
    4.2. BO1 Matches – Map Veto  
    4.3. BO3 Matches – Map Veto  
6. Withdraw  
7. Round Restore  
8. Tactical Pauses  
9. Forbidden Actions  
10. Prize Pool  
11. Valve Regional Standings  
12. Refund Policy  
13. Equipment and Venue Conduct  
14. Tournament Administration and Rule Interpretation  
15. Disclaimer

---

### **1\. General Provisions**

The tournament **“BC GAME MASTERS CHAMPIONSHIP \#2”** will be a **Counter-Strike 2** competition, held from **21 to 24 May** **(depending on the numbers of teams)** at **SAW Esports Arena, Vila Nova de Gaia**.

The competition will be **open to the Global World region through the Valve Regional Standings**, in accordance with Valve’s regional eligibility rules.

The tournament will feature **two stages**: **group stage** and **playoffs**, both played **offline (on LAN)**.

Example: **Group 1**

* Team A: Arena 1

* Team B: Arena 2

* Team C: Gaming Room 1

* Team D: Gaming Room 2

* Team E: Playground 1

* Team F: Playground 2

Each player must bring their **own peripherals** (keyboard, mouse, mousepad, sound card, headset & in-ears). Every game needs to be played with noise cancelling headphones and in-ears. 

If any player does not have all their equipment and cannot find a solution in time (**within 15 minutes from the scheduled match time**), that team will be sanctioned with a **loss of that match/map**.

---

### **2\. Registrations**

To register for the tournament, each team must fill out the forms.

The team registration will be handled by a **team representative**, who must fill in **all required fields**, otherwise the registration will be marked as **invalid**.

The **registration fee is 2000€ \+ VAT per team if applied**. Team registration will open May 2 at 17H30 GMT \+ 2\. 

**IMPORTANT:**

* The **first registered teams** will be selected to participate in the event.

* The **teams will be contacted** in order to proceed with the registration payment. As soon as the payment is completed, we will send a confirmation of the registration to the email of the team representative.

* All payments must be completed for the registration to be considered **complete**.

* If a team **misses the payment deadline** to confirm its registration, it will be **replaced** by a backup team according to the order of form submission.

* All **team captains** of the participating teams must sign a **liability statement** regarding **equipment damaged** due to misuse, negligence or theft.

* Only individuals **over 13 years of age** may register.

* Once a team has submitted his roster, a maximum of **two player changes will be permitted totally**. **Until 16 days** before the start of the event, teams may replace any player on the roster, provided. From **15 days until 7 days** before the start of the event, teams still can make roster changes however the **VRS core must remain unchanged**. **Within 7 days** of the tournament start, a roster emergency change are only permitted in exceptional circumstances and must be supported by valid and appropriate justification, subject to tournament administration approval.

* Teams will have a **15-minute grace period** from the scheduled match time to be ready to start the game. If they exceed this window, they will be sanctioned with a **default loss** and, consequently, defeat in that match. In **BO3 matches**, the teams will receive the same sanction for the map in question, and will have **an additional 15 minutes** to join the server and start the next map.

---

### **3\. Tournament Format**

**3.1 Group stage scoring system**

**Round-Robin.**

* Wining \- 3 points.  
* Loss \- 0 points.

**GSL.**

* Winning the game moves forward on bracket.  
* Losing the game go to the lower bracket or gets eliminated.

**3.2 Group stage tiebreaker**

If two or more teams finish the group stage with the same points, the following criteria will be applied in the order listed:

* Head-to-head result.  
* Round difference of all games played versus every team.  
* Fewer total round losses of all games played versus every team.  
* The higher initial seed used to seed the tournament.

**3.3 Format**

**In the case of having 8 teams.**

* Two groups will be done with the format GSL.   
* First game of group its played on BO1 format and the other games BO3 format.  
* Top 2 of each group go to the Playoffs.  
* The Playoffs will be single elimination and played on BO3 format, including the game of 3/4º place.   
* The competition will be played in 2 days.

**In the case of having 9 to 10 teams.**

* Two groups will be played, each game BO1, round-robin.   
* Top two teams from each group advance to the Playoffs.   
* The Playoffs will be single elimination and played on BO3 format, including the game of 3/4º place.   
* The competition will be played in 2 days.

**In the case of having 11 to 12 teams.**

* Two groups will be played, each game BO1, round-robin.   
* Top three teams from each group advance to the Playoffs.   
* Group Winners go directly to the semi-finals and the other four teams go to quarter-finals.  
* The Playoffs will be double elimination and played on BO3 format.  
* The competition will be played in 3 days.

**In the case of having 13 to 14 teams.**

* Two groups will be played, each game BO1, round-robin. The two VRS best teams will be placed directly on Playoffs.  
* Top three of each group go to the Playoffs.  
* The Playoffs will be double elimination and played on BO3 format.   
* The competition will be played in 4 days.

**In the case of having 15 to 16 teams.**

* Two groups will be played, each game BO1 round-robin. The four VRS best teams will be placed directly on Playoffs.  
* Top one of each group advance to the Playoffs.  
* Top two of Group A will play vs Top 3 of Group B and Top 3 of Group A will play vs Top 2 of Group B a BO3 match to decide the last two teams on Playoffs.  
* The Playoffs will be double elimination and played on BO3 format.  
* The competition will be played in 4 days.

---

### **4\. Tournament Schedule**

### The tournament schedule will depend on team number registered.  

The tournament will follow a **rolling schedule**, meaning matches may start earlier depending on the administration and on the progress of other ongoing matches.

Each team must be present for its **first matches 1 hour before** the scheduled start time.

---

### **5\. Settings and Maps**

#### **5.1. Server Configuration**

* Round time: **1:55 minutes**

* Freeze time: **15 seconds**

* Buy time: **20 seconds**

* Overtime start money: **$12,500**

* Overtime mode: **BO6**

* **Agent skins are not allowed**

* **Map pool**: Ancient, Overpass, Dust II, Inferno, Mirage, Nuke & Anubis

#### **5.2. BO1 Matches – Map Veto**

* Team A removes 1 map

* Team B removes 1 map

* Team A removes 1 map

* Team B removes 1 map

* Team A removes 1 map

* Team B removes 1 map

The **remaining map** will be the map to be played. 

A **knife round** will be played to determine which side each team starts on. The highest team seed used to see the tournament decides who starts banning.

#### **5.3. BO3 Matches – Map Veto**

* Team A removes 1 map

* Team B removes 1 map

* Team A picks 1 map

  * Team B chooses which side they start on

* Team B picks 1 map

  * Team A chooses which side they start on

* Team A removes 1 map

* Team B removes 1 map

The **remaining map** will be the **decider map** in case of a tie.

On the **last map**, a **knife round** will be played to determine which side each team starts on. The highest team seed used to see the tournament decides who starts banning.

---

### **6\. Withdraw**

Teams may withdraw from the tournament without penalty only if done **more than 15 days before the event start date**.

**From 15 days before the event onward**, any team that withdraws or fails to appear for a scheduled match will have that match counted as a loss (forfeit).

No exceptions will be allowed after this date, and tournament administration decisions are final and binding. 

---

### **7\. Round Restoration**

Round restoration may only be applied if the following conditions are met:

* No more than **60 seconds** have passed after the end of freeze time;

* No player has damage taken;

* The bomb has **not** been planted.

---

### **8\. Tactical Pauses**

Each team is entitled to a total of **3 tactical pauses**, each lasting **30 seconds**, with **1 additional tactical pause** being granted if the match goes to overtime. The coach will only be able to speak during tactical breaks and when switching sides.

All players must keep their **Steam status offline** while in a match.

---

### **9\. Forbidden Actions**

* The use of any **software or hardware** that offers an **unfair advantage** over the opponent is strictly prohibited.

* It is forbidden for any participant to use **exploits or bugs** present in the game to gain an unfair advantage over the opponent.

* Any communication between a participant and anyone **not involved** in the match is strictly prohibited.

The organization reserves the right to apply sanctions to the **final prize** and/or to **ban** players and/or teams from future competitions who demonstrate **unsportsmanlike or disrespectful behavior** towards other participants or towards the event staff and organization.

---

### **10\. Prize Pool**

### The **BC Game Masters Championship \#2** features a **total prize pool of $50,000 USDT**  **(ERC-20 on the Ethereum network) Crypto**. 

The total amount and distribution are fixed regardless of the final number of participating teams, provided the event takes place as scheduled.

* 1st Place: $30,000 USDT  
* 2nd Place: $10,000 USDT  
* 3rd Place: $5,000 USDT  
* 4th Place: $2,000 USDT  
* 5th–6th Place: $1,000 USDT  
* 7th–8th Place: $500 USDT

All prize amounts are listed in **USDT**.

The prize money will be paid to the **team** or its **representative**, defined at the time of registration, through **invoice**.

Prize payments will be made via **crypto transfer (ERC-20 on the Ethereum network)**. Payments will be issued **within ninety (90) days** after the conclusion of the event. The full prize amount for each placement will be paid in **one single transfer** to the designated recipient. The designated recipient must be either: the team organization, or the team captain or authorized representative. It is the team’s responsibility to: Provide correct and complete payment details and Agree internally on prize splits between players and coaches.

The organizer’s obligation is considered fulfilled once the prize has been transferred to the designated recipient.

### **Taxes & Legal Compliance**

Prize money will be awarded and disbursed in accordance with international laws.

* Any tax obligations arising from prize winnings are the **sole responsibility of the recipients**.

* The organizer may require invoices, identification documents, or payment forms before processing payouts.

Failure to provide required documentation may delay payment.

### **Disqualification & Forfeiture of Prizes** 

If a team is disqualified at any stage of the tournament, including during playoffs or after match completion, the organizer reserves the right to **forfeit the team’s claim to any prize money**, regardless of their final placement.

Disqualification due to cheating, match-fixing, roster violations, or severe rule breaches will automatically void prize eligibility.

---

### **11\. Valve Regional Standings (VRS)**

The **BC GAME MASTERS CHAMPIONSIP \#2**  will be compatible with the **Valve Regional Standings (VRS)** system, but **teams are not required to have VRS points** to participate.

* The competition will be **open to all regions**, in accordance with Valve’s general eligibility rules.

* Teams **without** Valve Regional Standings may freely participate and will be seeded using **alternative criteria** defined by the tournament administration.

* For teams that **do have** Valve Regional Standings, the **seeding** will take into account the **VRS ranking as of 4 May**.

* In order to assure the respective VRS position, the team must use the core present in the above mentioned ranking.

* Any changes to the Valve Regional Standings **after 4 May** will **not** affect the seeding for this competition.

* After consulting the VRS ranking and if there are still teams without seeding, the HLTV ranking from the same date as the VRS ranking will be used.

* If there are teams without VRS and HLTV ranks, the average faceit elo of the registered players at the end of the day of **4 May** will be used to determine their seed. If the player does not currently have a faceit elo, their last known rank will be used. If they have never had elo, it will be considered 1000\.

---

### **12\. Refund Policy**

The participation fee is non-refundable, except in the case that the tournament is cancelled.

No refunds will be issued for withdrawals, no-shows, or inability to participate for reasons not caused by the tournament operator.

---

### **13\. Disclaimer**

All participants must treat all event equipment, furniture, and venue facilities with appropriate care and respect. 

Any participant who intentionally or negligently mistreats or misuses equipment, tables, chairs, computers, monitors, or any other event property may be subject to penalties. A minimum fine of €500 will be applied for any misuse of event equipment or facilities. If such behavior results in physical damage, the responsible participant and/or team may be required to cover the full cost of repair or replacement, in addition to any competitive penalties imposed by the tournament administration.

---

### **14\. Equipment and Venue Conduct**

The Tournament Administration reserves the right to **interpret, modify, or add rules** if situations arise that are not explicitly covered by this rulebook.

The **Head Administrator** may adjust or enforce rules at any time in order to preserve the integrity, fairness, and smooth operation of the competition.

All decisions made by the Tournament Administration are **final and binding**.

---

### **15\. Disclaimer**

All **broadcast rights** of the competition belong to the **organization**. The organization may assign these rights to **third parties**.

By registering for the competition, teams agree to **grant image rights** of their members to the competition organizer. The use of the image is intended for the **promotion of the competition** or for the creation of **content for the competition partners** or **future competitions**.

No team or participant may refuse the **broadcast of their matches** by the organization.
